Notice Of Motion And Motion For Order Granting Preference In Setting Case For Trial, And Extending Discovery Cutoff

MINI MINUTES:
No appearances and tentative ruling adopted: Plaintiffs' Motion for Order Granting Preference in Setting Case for Trial, and Extending Discovery Cutoff is GRANTED pursuant to C.C.P. 36(a). Intervenor Great American Insurance Company filed an opposition. The trial date is October 19, 2020 at 11:15 a.m. in Department 503. Last day pursuant to C.C.P. 36(f) is October 29, 2020. Local Rule 20 shall be followed by the parties as well as all San Francisco Local Rules and California Rules of Court. The last day for hearing summary judgment/adjudication motions is October 15, 2020. Summary judgment/adjudication motions shall be brought on regular notice pursuant to the relevant provisions of the C.C.P., unless the parties stipulate otherwise. The Court will allow a maximum of four summary judgment/adjudication motions per day to be calendared in this action. Before a party files and serves such motion, it must contact the Court's Calendar Clerk to make a reservation. Time to respond to written discovery that has not yet been served is shortened to 20 days. For written discovery that has already been served, responses are due within 20 days of this hearing or by the date determined by the C.C.P., whichever is earlier. If there is any kind of issue/dispute that requires meet and confer, such meet and confer shall not be made via email or letter. It shall occur in person or via telephone. Electronic service is considered the equivalent of personal service. Fact discovery cut-off date is October 2, 2020. Expert discovery cut-off date is October 16, 2020. All bankruptcy documents shall be turned over to defendants no later than July 31, 2020. If plaintiffs submit documents to bankruptcy trusts after this date, they must notify defendants of this no later than five days after submission. The fact discovery cut-off does not apply to bankruptcy documents.
(Mini Minutes continued, see next entry)

RULING: HEARING REGARDING PREFERENCE PURSUANT TO CCP 36(A)
Hearing held and tentative ruling adopted: On January 30, 2019, the Court of Appeal of the State of California, First District, Division Two issued a peremptory writ of mandate requiring this Court to set aside and vacate its August 7, 2018 order denying Plaintiff David Ellis' motion for trial preference pursuant to Code of Civil Procedure section 36(a) and to set a trial date with preference within 120 days of the Court of Appeal's opinion. Pursuant to the January 30, 2019 peremptory writ of mandate, the Court hereby vacates its August 7, 2018 order denying Plaintiff David Ellis' motion for trial preference. The Court enters a new order setting this matter for trial preference pursuant to Code of Civil Procedure section 36(a).

Plaintiff's motion for preference is granted pursuant to Code of Civil Procedure section 36(a). All previous orders setting trial and holding dates are vacated. The new trial date is May 20, 2019 at 11:15 a.m. in Department 503. Last day pursuant to Code of Civil Procedure section 36(f) and the Court of Appeal's peremptory writ of mandate is May 30, 2019. Discovery remains closed.

Plaintiff to make a settlement demand on defendants no later than April 5, 2019. Responses to the demand are due no later than April 19, 2019. The parties are ordered to provide confidential settlement demands stating their best and final offers. These confidential demands must be submitted to Commissioner Pang Ly via electronic mail by May 10, 2019 at 12:00 p.m. Mandatory settlement conference with only the attorneys present and final decision makers available by phone is set for Thursday, May 16, 2019 at 9:30 a.m. in 603. Order signed in open court. Judge: Cynthia Ming-mei Lee, Clerk: A. Mok. Not reported. (503/CML)
